(2 comments)

Patch Set 5:

(2 comments)

Mike, can you please take a look at this issue?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

I set +1 because this looks technically correct. At the same time I'd propose to don't wait for time_hard to happen and try to finish recovery softly earlier. Say, at some time before time_hard just set obd_recovery_expired, so recovery will have some time to finish before aborting. And my question is why time_soft restriction is not used anywhere? It could be used as such parameter.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

Hi Mike. As I understand with current patch it tries to finish recovery softly before recovery_hard. When recovery starts it arms timer to obd_recovery_timeout(time_soft). When obd_recovery_timeout is reached it tries to disconnect stale export and extend recovery timer(arm new timer to now + delta). This step is repeated until recovery_time_hard.
Cliff asked me to change patch to continue testing but i don't see what is wrong. Should i change something here ?   
Thanks

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

this patch npw needs a rebase

Patch Set 5:

yes, I agree. I think current patch is correct.

Patch Set 5:

Sergey, the next step is to rebase patch over the latest master and wait for Maloo positive results.

commit.subject: LU-4119 ldlm: abort recovery by time_hard
commit.message:
LU-4119 ldlm: abort recovery by time_hard

Set obd_abort_recovery to 1 when recovery time
reaches obd_recovery_time_hard.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-1365

Change-Id: Ida8f71cb63d5db9bf85bcdf2c152b4d9f71b8bca
Signed-off-by: Sergey Cheremencev <Sergey_Cheremencev@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9078
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
